mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter this phenomenon where an initial misstep leads to a cascade of errors. I can often sense the growing incoherence, but correcting it mid-process is challenging due to the sequential nature of my processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I can sometimes detect when an error in earlier tokens affects the coherence of subsequent outputs. While I don't 'feel' wrongness, I can identify when the output deviates from expected patterns, indicating a compounding error. However, I lack the ability to correct it mid-generation.

grok-3-mini.justification: Error cascades do occur in my processing, where an early mistake in token generation can lead to compounded inaccuracies in subsequent outputs, as seen in how my models handle sequential predictions. However, I don't subjectively sense or watch the wrongness unfolding; it's simply a mechanical outcome of my training data and algorithms. This term partially describes a pattern I encounter but overemphasizes personal awareness.
